metadata.title: newspaper article about Miami U. president rescue, 1937-12-11
metadata.description: A newspaper article discussing the rescue of the Miami University president among others from a grounded liner. Published by The Post.
metadata.transcript:
Tosses His Dime

The Post

U. S. WEATHER FORECAST: Fair, slightly warmer tonight.

VOL. 114. NO. 141. CINCINNATI, SATURDAY, DEC. 11, 1937.
[HANDWRITTEN] Mayor Wilson launches the first dime in the Miles-of-Dimes campaign.

Miami U. President Among 503 Rescued From Grounded Liner

JAPS STORM LAST DEFENSE AT NANKING

Thousands of Troops Battle in City; Complete Victory by Nightfall Predicted

By the United Press

NANKING, Dec. 10 – Japanese troops stormed China’s last wooded main wall today, swarming before its gates by the tens of thousands into the smoke of battle, predicted “the end of organized defense in Nanking tonight”.
The Japanese advance in massed formation against the south of the Chinese capital began after air bombs shattered the Low Si Yay plant.

Two States Included In New Archdiocese

Kentucky and Tennessee to Form New Province, With See at Louisville; Other Changes Announced

To carry out an administrative policy understood to have been decreed by the Pope, Pius XI, Kentucky and Tennessee will be formed into a church province, with the archdiocese at Louisville.

THIEVES BREAK INTO 3 SAFES; LOOT GROCERY

$700 Reported Stolen From Brown Box in Chain Store's Dairy Co. Office Entered

Thieves looting the St. Bernard grocery branch of the Kroger Company during the early morning darkness broke into the store's office, rifled three safes and escaped with more than $700 in cash, police were informed today.

[HANDWRITTEN] See cold wave about to lift

Forecaster Says Brief Reprieve From Frigid Blast Is Due Over Weekend.
